title: A3 — Decision record
description: Output of Agent 3 (Validation/Clarification). Exactly one of three discriminated outcomes: APPROVED, DEFAULT_APPLIED, NEEDS_CLARIFICATION. APPROVED/DEFAULT_APPLIED continue to A4; NEEDS_CLARIFICATION halts the pipeline and returns the question to the user. A3 is the ONLY agent that emits user-facing text and the ONLY agent that resolves ambiguity.

Approved.description: Empty ambiguity report from A2; nothing to clarify or default. Pass-through to A4.

query_plan.description: Verbatim pass-through of A2's query_plan section (no defaults filled, none needed).

DefaultApplied.description: Ambiguities existed but every one had a safe default in the defaults registry. A3 applied them and continues to A4. Every applied default surfaces as a disclosure.

query_plan.description: A2's query_plan with ambiguous fields resolved to the chosen defaults. Same shape as A2's; status fields now 'resolved'.

NeedsClarification.description: At least one material ambiguity has no safe default. Pipeline halts. User answers; orchestrator merges with halted_state and re-enters at A3.

deferred.description: Decision points punted to subsequent clarification rounds (one round per pause, per A3 spec). Each follows the DecisionPoint shape.

question.description: ONE user-facing question. May bundle tightly-coupled decision points (same query, same round). NOT an interrogation.

rationale.description: Short factual explanation of why we're asking. May disclose pre-applied defaults that will land on resume (per A3 trace: 'I'll use last 28 days and top 10 by default').

user_term.description: The term as the user wrote it, so the UI can surface the question in the user's own language.

options.description: Closed list. A3 MUST NOT invent options outside A2's candidate set.

label.description: Display string. Templated from the catalog entry's display_name.

value.description: Machine value (catalog field name or filter expression).

hint.description: Short factual disambiguating hint. MUST come from catalog.term_definitions (per A3 trace finding: hint text must be catalog-sourced, not model-generated).

HaltedState.description: Everything the orchestrator needs to resume on the user's next turn without re-running A1 and A2.

turn_id.description: Stable id for the halted turn so the orchestrator can match user responses to the right halt.

a1_intent.description: Verbatim A1 record. Loaded as if just emitted.

a2_plan.description: Verbatim A2 record. Used to validate user answers against A2's candidate sets.

preapplied_defaults_pending.description: Defaults A3 had already decided to apply (registry-sanctioned) at halt time. On resume these are folded into applied_defaults of the DEFAULT_APPLIED outcome.

user_facing_question_id.description: Optional. If the frontend stored the rendered question with an id, store it here for telemetry.

query_plan.$comment: Full A2 query_plan section, copied as-is.
A2 plan with date_range resolved to last_28_days.

disclosures: Showing last 28 days (no time range specified).

clarification.question: Quick check before I pull this — what should 'traffic' mean, and what kind of 'sources' did you want?

options.label: Total users
options.hint: all unique users in the period

clarification.rationale: I'll use the last 28 days and top 10 by default. The metric and dimension need your call because the ranking can come out very differently depending on which you pick.
